title: Assessing AtriCure (ATRC) Valuation After Prolonged Share Price Weakness
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Event overview and recent share performance

AtriCure (ATRC) has been drawing attention after a period of weak share performance, with the stock showing a 3% decline over the past month and a 32% decline over the past 3 months.

For investors, that price pressure sits against a backdrop of annual revenue of US$534.5 million and an annual net loss of US$11.4 million. This raises questions about how the current valuation lines up with the underlying business.

See our latest analysis for AtriCure.

Looking over a longer stretch, the share price return shows a sharp 31.9% decline over 3 months and a 28.1% decline year to date, while the 1 year total shareholder return of a 10.8% decline points to pressure that has been building rather than a short term wobble.

If this kind of reset has you reassessing opportunities in healthcare, it can be useful to compare AtriCure with other names using our screener of 34 healthcare AI stocks

With AtriCure shares under pressure, and annual revenue at US$534.5 million and a market value of about US$1.4b, the key question is whether the recent slide leaves an undervalued medtech name or if the market is already factoring in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 47% Undervalued

At a last close of $28.22 against a narrative fair value of about $52.78, AtriCure sits at a wide gap that hinges on ambitious growth and profitability assumptions.

The successful completion of enrollment in the LeAAPS clinical trial, as well as early activation of the BoxX-NoAF study, are set to substantially increase AtriCure's total addressable market if positive outcomes lead to expanded indications for stroke prevention and preventative cardiac surgery in underserved populations, which will support future revenue and earnings growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue trajectory and margin lift would need to materialise to back a premium future earnings multiple and still arrive at that fair value? The most followed narrative sets a detailed path for sales growth, profitability and valuation that is far from conservative. The key driver is how quickly earnings turn positive and then scale. The full story is in how all those moving parts are combined.

Result: Fair Value of $52.78 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this upbeat narrative still runs into some clear hurdles, including pressure from pulsed field ablation competitors and the risk that high R&D spending fails to translate into meaningful revenue.

Story Continues

Find out about the key risks to this AtriCure narrative.

Another View: Cash Flows Point a Very Different Way

While the narrative fair value suggests AtriCure could be worth about $52.78 per share, the SWS DCF model comes out very differently, with an estimated future cash flow value of just $1.62 per share. That is a wide gap. Which story do you trust more with your money?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.ATRC Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026
